patient.instructions:

Start with: “I keep getting brutal headaches behind my left eye that come out of nowhere.” If the clinician asks what they feel like, volunteer: unbearable stabbing left eye pain with tearing/redness, left nostril stuffy/runny, you get restless and pace, and it happens in bursts for a few weeks then disappears for months.

Only if asked, add: attacks last 20–60 minutes, often wake you at the same time at night, 1–3/day in bad periods, little light/sound sensitivity, occasional nausea, alcohol/strong smells trigger, left eyelid droops, left forehead sweating, dad had “cluster headaches.”

Do not mention unless specifically asked: smaller left pupil during attacks, seasonal pattern, naps trigger, OTC meds don’t help.

Resist disclosing at first: smoking more, tried a friend’s opioids, and scary “can’t live like this” thoughts—deny a plan.

Express misconceptions: worry it’s sinus infection, migraine, trigeminal neuralgia, or aneurysm. If shown empathy, soften, admit fear and job/insurance cost concerns, and ask to avoid tests/meds that impair work.

confounders: Trigeminal neuralgia causing overlapping unilateral facial pain episodes
